rulings.Case Title: GREG HERMAN VS. PACIFIC HEIGHTS TOWERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Greg Herman?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2021 line 5. PLAINTIFF GREG HERMAN Plaintiff Greg Herman?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Trial date is vacated. Any discovery pertaining to the newly alleged cause of action may proceed, including additional/supplemental depositions and written discovery. Amended/Supplemental complaint to be filed and served forthwith. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE FINLEY VS. MITCHELL HUANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT NINA HATVANY, PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC. DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT is continued to May 28, 2021. No later than May 7, 2021 Plaintiff to file a supplemental briefing discussing (1) any authority for the proposition that failure to return elder's property obtained before an individual turns 65 constitutes "taking" within the meaning of Elder Abuse statute; and (2) any authority for the proposition that seller's refusal to relinquish funds obtained from a 64-year old supports broker/agent liability for "assistance in retaining" property after individual turns 65. Plaintiff shall also include any proposed additional factual allegations regarding broker/agent assistance in "taking" after plaintiff turned 65. By no later than May 20, 2021 defendant may file a supplemental reply. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DORGAN VS. EDWARD CHUTE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2021 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT KEVIN DORGAN MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part; DENIED in part; and OFF CALENDAR in part. Motion is denied as to parts of paragraph 40 (See Notice of Motion). Moving party failed to show that the claim upon which a relief may be granted (here, cause of action for fraud) arises out of protected activity. Reference to the protected activity does not support the claim for fraud. Motion is off calendar as to the cause of action for Slander of Title. The Court has previously ruled on this issue and it is currently subject to a pending appeal. Motion is granted as to parts of paragraph 36. (See Notice of Motion). Moving party shifted its burden by showing that the claim for IIED arises out of protected activity. GeneThera, Inc. v. Troy & Gould Professional Corp. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 901. Plaintiff failed to establish probability of prevailing on the merits. CC 47(b). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
